name: episteme
description: A native Android document reader application built with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose.
readme_summary: Episteme Reader is a native Android application for reading various document and e-book formats offline. It focuses on privacy and offers features like annotations, text-to-speech, and customizable reading experiences.
key_features: Supports PDF, EPUB, MOBI, AZW3, Markdown, HTML, and plain text.
Ink and text annotations for PDFs.
Native parsing for MOBI/AZW3 and EPUB.
Customizable e-book reading (font size, line spacing, margins, custom fonts).
primary_use_case: A versatile, privacy-conscious offline document and e-book reader for Android devices.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian standing in a cozy, dimly lit library, holding a magical, open book. From the book, different types of documents and e-books (PDFs, EPUBs, MOBI) are gracefully emerging and arranging themselves neatly on floating shelves. The librarian is pointing a gentle finger, and a subtle glow emanates from the book, indicating organization and accessibility. Small, stylized icons representing annotations and text-to-speech float around the scene. The overall mood is calm and intellectual, with a focus on the seamless integration of diverse reading materials. The art style should be a warm, digital painting with clear outlines and a touch of fantasy.

name: claude-swarm
description: MCP server for orchestrating parallel Claude Code worker swarms with protocol-based behavioral governance, persistent state, and real-time monitoring dashboard
readme_summary: Claude Swarm is a TypeScript-based MCP server designed to orchestrate parallel Claude Code worker swarms for long-running autonomous coding sessions. It features persistent state management, parallel worker execution using tmux and isolated git worktrees, and protocol-based behavioral governance to enforce constraints during runtime.
primary_use_case: Orchestrating and managing parallel, long-running autonomous coding sessions using Claude Code workers, with robust state persistence, behavioral governance, and real-time monitoring.
cover_image_prompt: A conductor leading a symphony orchestra, where each musician is an AI (represented by a stylized Claude robot head) playing a different instrument (coding tasks). The conductor, representing the MCP server, uses a glowing baton to direct the flow of music (code execution) and maintain harmony (protocol adherence). Sheet music on the stands shows snippets of code and progress bars. In the background, a digital dashboard displays real-time performance metrics and a timeline of completed tasks. The scene is set in a futuristic, clean concert hall with subtle digital particle effects emanating from the AI musicians. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant, contrasting colors to highlight the organized chaos and sophisticated orchestration.

name: scantailor-advanced
description: ScanTailor Advanced is the version that merges the features of the ScanTailor Featured and ScanTailor Enhanced versions, brings new ones and fixes.
readme_summary: ScanTailor Advanced is an interactive post-processing tool for scanned pages, merging and enhancing features from previous versions. It automates tasks like page splitting, deskewing, border adjustment, and content selection, preparing raw scans for digital assembly into formats like PDF or DjVu.
primary_use_case: Post-processing scanned documents to prepare them for digital assembly into formats like PDF or DjVu.
cover_image_prompt: A meticulous bookbinder's workshop, where stacks of raw, slightly messy scanned pages are being fed into a magical, intricate machine. The machine, subtly branded with 'ScanTailor Advanced', uses glowing beams and precise robotic arms to automatically split, straighten, and crop each page, removing unwanted borders and precisely selecting the content. The output is a neat stack of perfectly aligned, ready-to-assemble book pages. The scene is bathed in warm, natural light, with subtle digital particles and UI elements hinting at the software's functionality. Rendered in a detailed 3D isometric illustration style.

name: openlibrary
description: One webpage for every book ever published!
readme_summary: Open Library is an open-source project aiming to create a web page for every book ever published. It functions as an editable library catalog, allowing for community contributions to build a comprehensive database of literary works.
key_features: Web page for every book ever published
Developer documentation and quick start guides
primary_use_case: To build and maintain a comprehensive, community-driven catalog of all books ever published, making information about them accessible online.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, old librarian meticulously organizing an infinite, ever-expanding library where each book has its own glowing, interactive digital page. The librarian is placing a new book onto a shelf, and as it settles, its digital page springs to life with information. Around the scene, subtle holographic interfaces display book covers, author names, and publication dates, with small 'edit' icons hinting at community contributions. The overall mood is one of discovery and collective knowledge building, rendered in a warm, inviting digital painting style with clear outlines and a focus on the vastness of the collection.

name: pathway
description: Python ETL framework for stream processing, real-time analytics, LLM pipelines, and RAG.
readme_summary: Pathway is a Python ETL framework designed for efficient stream processing, real-time analytics, and building LLM pipelines, including RAG. It offers a flexible Python API that works seamlessly with ML libraries, supporting both batch and streaming data for development and production. Its core is a scalable Rust engine that enables incremental computation, multithreading, multiprocessing, and distributed processing, with easy deployment options via Docker and Kubernetes.
key_features: Handles both batch and streaming data
primary_use_case: Building and deploying real-time data processing pipelines, analytics, and LLM applications using a unified Python API.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, bustling city where streams of data (represented by glowing, colorful rivers) flow into a central, intricate clockwork mechanism. This mechanism, subtly branded with the 'Pathway' logo, efficiently processes and transforms the data streams into organized, useful outputs like real-time dashboards and intelligent chatbot interfaces. Small, stylized Python code snippets float around the mechanism, indicating the programming language. The overall scene is clean, modern, and dynamic, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a bright, optimistic color palette, conveying seamless data flow and intelligent processing.
